name: Qwen3-Max
archDisplay: MoE, exact expert configuration undisclosed (>1T total params)
note: Alibaba's biggest model yet, over 1T params (exact undisclosed) trained on 36T tokens; API-only preview.

results.notes: Tau2-Bench, surpassing Claude Opus 4 and DeepSeek V3.1

SuperGPQA, Qwen3-Max-Instruct (Preview) official launch chart (same felloai transcription as the AIME/Arena-Hard rows; chart also shows Qwen3-235B 62.6, Kimi K2 57.2, Claude Opus 4 56.5). llm-stats lists the GA release at 65.1. Corrects a prior 81.4 (dev.to) that misread the AIME bar (GA AIME transcriptions cite 81.6).

AIME 2025, Qwen3-Max-Instruct (Preview, non-thinking), official launch chart (also transcribed by localllm.in). Some GA-release transcriptions cite 81.6; Thinking variant later reported 100 with tools + test-time compute.
Arena-Hard v2, Qwen3-Max-Instruct (Preview), official launch chart (also transcribed by localllm.in).
LiveCodeBench v6, Qwen3-Max-Instruct (Preview) launch chart. Conflicting GA-era transcriptions cite 74.8 (e.g. apidog); no official text source resolves it.

GPQA Diamond (scientific reasoning), Qwen3-Max-Preview (non-reasoning). Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index v4.1 breakdown, confirmed via direct browser fetch with page-title verified as 'Qwen3 Max (Preview)' and FAQ confirming 'released on September 5, 2025' -- exact match to this catalog entry. Score read off AA's evaluation chart (rounded to whole percent); a third-party aggregator (designforonline.com, citing AA+HF) separately reports 76.4% for the later Sept-23 GA 'Qwen3-Max' release, consistent within rounding.

Humanity's Last Exam, Qwen3-Max-Preview (non-reasoning). Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index v4.1 breakdown, same title/date-verified fetch as the GPQA entry. AA's chart shows 9% (rounded); designforonline.com separately cites 9.3% for the GA Sept-23 release, consistent.

IFBench (instruction following), Qwen3-Max-Preview (non-reasoning). Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index v4.1 breakdown, same title/date-verified fetch. AA's chart shows 48%, exactly matching the independently cited 48% for the GA Sept-23 release on designforonline.com.

LMArena (Chatbot Arena) Text leaderboard Elo rating for model id 'qwen3-max-preview' (linked to Alibaba Cloud Model Studio docs), rank #5 at this Nov 13 2025 archive.org snapshot, ~25,932 votes, +/-5. Score fluctuated narrowly (1432-1438) across multiple archived snapshots from Nov 2025 through Jan 2026; this is the earliest well-populated post-launch snapshot.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real: preview announced Sept 5, 2025 (X/Alibaba_Qwen, MarkTechPost, Cybernews, Interesting Engineering all corroborate), over 1T total params (undisclosed exact figure) trained on 36T tokens, MoE architecture, closed/API-only via Alibaba Cloud/DashScope -- all confirmed. SWE-bench Verified 69.6 and Tau2-Bench 74.8 confirmed verbatim via direct fetch of the cited alibabacloud.com blog. SuperGPQA 81.4 confirmed verbatim via direct fetch of the cited dev.to article. Pricing ($1.2/$6 per M, 0-32K tier) confirmed exactly via Alibaba Cloud Model Studio official pricing page. No changes made.

2026-07-16: Checked mmlu/math/humaneval/mmmu core-benchmark gaps for Qwen3-Max. (1) mmlu: not in the official launch materials (alibabacloud.com blog and felloai.com launch-chart transcription list only SuperGPQA, AIME25, LiveCodeBench v6, Arena-Hard v2, LiveBench, SWE-bench, Tau2-Bench -- no plain MMLU), nor on Artificial Analysis (which tracks its own suite -- GPQA/HLE/IFBench/etc, no MMLU) or vals.ai (which lists MMLU-Pro, a distinct benchmark/slug, but never plain MMLU) for either the Preview or GA release. No Qwen3-Max-specific technical report exists (it's closed/API-only) to cross-reference. Treated as a legitimate saturated-legacy-benchmark omission, not a gap to guess at -- omitted_no_evidence. (2) math: same three source types checked (official launch chart, Artificial Analysis, vals.ai) -- none report the classic MATH (Hendrycks) benchmark; Qwen3-Max's own materials use AIME25 (80.6, already recorded) and SuperGPQA instead, consistent with a lab choosing harder/newer math evals over the now-near-saturated MATH set -- omitted_no_evidence. (3) humaneval: same check across official launch chart, Artificial Analysis, and vals.ai -- none list HumanEval for Qwen3-Max (Preview or GA); the model's own coding evidence is via SWE-bench Verified (69.6) and LiveCodeBench v6 (57.5), both already recorded -- omitted_no_evidence. (4) mmmu: MMMU is a vision-multimodal benchmark; Qwen3-Max is confirmed text-only (modalities: [\"text\"], capabilities.vision: false in this catalog entry, corroborated by independent coverage explicitly describing Qwen3-Max/Qwen3.7-Max as \"text-only (no vision input)\") -- a genuine architectural absence, not a search failure -- omitted_no_evidence.

2026-07-18 (round 22): Re-chased the mmmu gap independently rather than resting on the 2026-07-16 note. Confirmed via corpus fields (modalities: ["text"], capabilities.vision: false) and cross-checked with 4+ independent methods: (1) llm-stats.com's own MMMU leaderboard, fetched directly -- Qwen3-Max does not appear anywhere on it; every Qwen-family entry present is a distinct multimodal-branded model (Qwen3.6 Plus #1 @86.0, Qwen3.5-122B-A10B, Qwen3.6-27B, Qwen3.5-27B, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B, Qwen2.5-VL variants, QvQ-72B, Qwen2.5-Omni-7B) -- never a "Max"-branded model. (2) designforonline.com's Qwen3-Max review page, fetched directly -- lists MMLU-Pro but no MMMU row, and its capabilities section lists only Tool Use/Function Calling, no vision/multimodal. (3) Web search on Qwen3-Max/Qwen3.7-Max vision capability -- multiple independent sources confirm a consistent Alibaba naming convention where "Max"-tier models are explicitly text-only across generations ("Qwen3.7-Max is text-in, text-out only... No vision capabilities whatsoever"), while multimodal input is reserved for sibling "Plus"/"VL" models (Qwen3.7-Plus, Qwen3-VL, Qwen2.5-VL) released alongside them. (4) Sibling-model technical report check -- the Qwen3-VL Technical Report (arXiv 2511.21631), which is the source of Qwen-family MMMU/MathVista results in this era, covers the separate Qwen3-VL line only; it does not cite Qwen3-Max. One search snippet surfaced an ambiguous "Qwen3-Max scores 85.0 on MMMU" claim, but tracing it back showed it was a search-summarizer conflation with Qwen3-VL/Qwen3.6-Plus figures, not a real score attributed to Qwen3-Max by any primary source. Conclusion unchanged and reinforced: mmmu is architecturally inapplicable for Qwen3-Max (confirmed text-only model) -- omitted_no_evidence, no new score exists to add.
